sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I need to analyze Level 2 and figure out the mechanics. Let me look at the segmentation data:
Key objects in Level 2:
- Blue squares (b, 16px each): at rows 16-19, 24-27, 36-39, 44-47 (all at cols 0-3)
- Black bars (B): rows 20-23 (cols 0-55), rows 40-43 (cols 0-27 and 30-43)
- Light green strips (N): rows 40-43, cols 28-29 (8px); rows 52-57, cols 8-9 (12px)
- Charcoal (c, 16px): rows 52-57, cols 10-13
- White areas: top-left, middle, bottom-left
- Gray area: right side
The layout is different from Level 1. In Level 1, blue squares were on the right edge. In Level 2, they're on the left edge.
The charcoal is at rows 52-57, cols 10-13, next to the light green strip at cols 8-9.
Looking at the ASCII:
- Row 44: 'GGGGGGGGGGGGGGGG' - all gray in the charcoal area
- The charcoal is embedded in the gray area
In Level 1, the goal was to move the charcoal to the leftmost position. The charcoal moved by clicking blue squares.
In Level 2, the blue squares are on the LEFT. Maybe I need to click them to move the charcoal in some direction. Let me try clicking on the blue squares to see what happens.
Looking at the blue squares:
1. b at rows 16-19, cols 0-3 (top)
2. b at rows 24-27, cols 0-3
3. b at rows 36-39, cols 0-3
4. b at rows 44-47, cols 0-3 (bottom)
In Level 1, clicking the top blue square moved the charcoal right, and clicking the bottom blue square moved it left. In Level 2, the blue squares are on the left, so maybe the mechanics are different.
Let me try clicking on the top blue square first to see what happens. I'll click at (18, 2) which is in the middle of the top blue square.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Level 2: Blue squares on LEFT edge. Charcoal at rows 52-57, cols 10-13
# Try clicking top blue square to see effect
print("Clicking top blue square at (18, 2)")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 18, 'col': 2}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c':
        print(f"  Charcoal: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'P':
        print(f"  Pink: {n['pixels']}px")

# Check charcoal position
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"\nCharcoal area (rows 50-58, cols 6-16):")
for r in range(50, 58):
    print(f"  {r}: '{ascii_lines[r][6:16]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Clicking top blue square at (18, 2)
  Pink: 63px
  Charcoal: pixels=1, boundary=[[0, 63]]
  Charcoal: pixels=16, boundary=[[52, 10], [52, 11], [53, 11], [54, 12], [54, 13], [55, 13], [55, 12], [56, 11], [57, 11], [57, 10]]

Charcoal area (rows 50-58, cols 6-16):
  50: 'WWGGGGGGGG'
  51: 'WWGGGGGGGG'
  52: 'WWNNccGGGG'
  53: 'WWNNccGGGG'
  54: 'WWNNccccGG'
  55: 'WWNNccccGG'
  56: 'WWNNccGGGG'
  57: 'WWNNccGGGG'
